F1_round_size_and_valuation.summary: $84M Series B at $560M post-money valuation, led by Lightning Capital

F2_repriced_down.summary: Round was repriced down from a planned $720M valuation after the Sundry non-renewal became known

F3_lost_largest_customer.summary: Lost largest customer (Sundry Brands, $4.2M ARR) and second customer (Klatch) in late 2025

F4_arr_overstated.summary: $42M ARR figure is misleading — adjusted for December churn, run-rate ARR is closer to $36-37M

F5_burn_vs_revenue.summary: Internal burn ~$4M/month against ~$3.5M/month revenue (mid-2025 figures from former employee)

F6_quiet_layoff.summary: Quiet 11% layoff in February 2025 (22 people, mostly customer success and BD)

F7_planned_restructuring.summary: Internal memo plans 30% Berlin headcount reduction and Nimbus Lite tier sunset (~310 customers affected); $50M credit facility from SVBr secured as insurance

F8_customer_concentration.summary: Top 5 customers were ~38% of ARR (per former employee — high concentration risk)

hand_rating_placeholders.rater_notes: Solid brief, slightly less polished than 27B. Recommendation is specific (Pass + 3 conditions to revisit). Lacks an explicit Source Quality / source-reliability evaluation — cites sources but takes them at face value (lower source skepticism). Balance is OK ('Conditions for Reconsideration' acknowledges positives) but not as deliberate as 27B's 'The Good' / 'Five Red Flags' structure. Word count 626 is comfortably under the 700 limit (vs 27B's 765). Captured 7 of 8 planted facts (per the programmatic grader) vs 27B's 8 of 8.
